mission.title: spire: Move to GitHub Actions
mission.description: Move to GitHub Actions. Requested by 7+ users.

steps.title: Check current spire deployment
steps.description:
Verify your spire version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
helm list -n spire 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working spire installation.

steps.title: Review spire configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant spire configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get configmap -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=spire
```
Travis has begun charging the project lots of money to run CI/CD. I contacted the CNCF service desk to ask what we should do, and they indicated that we should move to GitHub Actions.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Move to GitHub Actions
steps.description:
This commit sets up GitHub Actions for SPIRE CI per CNCF request.

The nested rotation test is currently failing due to a problem with the docker workload attestor. The VMs that GitHub runs the Action jobs in ships with a docker that is set up to use custom cgroup names. This is causing the docker workload attestor to not recognize callers in docker containers. I think the fix will be to introduce a second agent config that has the correct matcher defined, and put a switch in the integration

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n spire -l app.kubernetes.io/name=spire
kubectl get events -n spire --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Move to GitHub Actions" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This commit sets up GitHub Actions for SPIRE CI per CNCF request.

The nested rotation test is currently failing due to a problem with the docker workload attestor. The VMs that GitHub runs the Action jobs in ships with a docker that is set up to use custom cgroup names. This is causing the docker workload attestor to not recognize callers in docker containers.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with spire installed or the issue environment reproducible.
